title: Expedition route chapter phase
summary: Sixty-seventh structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-4 wave pushes one route family fully into expedition-grade chapter territory, then adds one compact return-page follow-on through the live notebook seam.

execution_order.why_now: The Route v2 loop is ready for one fuller chapter-grade conversion.

execution_order.goal: Turn `treeline-low-fell` into a four-leg edge-line chapter.
execution_order.why_now: The late routes-page capstone already points into `ROOT HOLLOW`, so deepening it is the cleanest way to make an ordinary route family feel expedition-grade.
The outing should feel bigger in structure, not harsher in genre.
A stronger chapter wants a more satisfying but still compact return-page close.
The notebook seam is the safest place to make the chapter close feel authored without widening the shell.
The close should feel richer without spending the generic stop cue or overgrowing the strip.

guardrails: Do not add combat, failure meters, or a bigger quest shell.
Reuse current Route v2, support, and notebook-return seams.
Keep the outing calm, authored, and place-specific.
acceptance_focus: At least one route family feels expedition-grade.
The return-page close feels satisfying and compact.
Lane 4 keeps the cozy naturalist direction intact.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-expedition-route-chapter-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-low-fell-chapter-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-note-tabs-chapter-close-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's later long-push wave after the process-and-filing work: `treeline-low-fell` is already the fuller four-leg chapter, so the next move is one compact note-tabs-only chapter-close line on the completed `edge-pattern-line` return page, keeping the generic stop cue, atlas note, and later Root Hollow or season-close states unchanged.
